summary: Null customer_id values in bronze.sales caused NOT NULL constraint violation when fct_sales model attempted to insert rows into gold.fct_sales. 6 out of 20 rows had empty customer_id in the source CSV.
root_cause: Source CSV contained rows with missing customer_id. No validation existed at the ingestion layer to reject or flag null key values. The silver_sales model cast NULL to NULL integer without filtering, and the fct_sales model contract enforces NOT NULL on customer_id.
evidence_summary: SQL null check found 6 rows with NULL customer_id in bronze.sales. Code inspection of fct_sales.sql confirmed LEFT JOIN on customer_id without null filter. dbt model contract on gold.fct_sales enforces NOT NULL on customer_id column.
fix_applied: Added WHERE customer_id IS NOT NULL filter to silver_sales.sql before type cast. Added not_null dbt test on bronze.sales.customer_id.
prevention_added: Added ingestion validation step that rejects CSVs with more than 1% null values in key columns. Added row-level quality gate between bronze and silver layers.

summary: Duplicate order_id values in bronze.sales caused inflated revenue metrics in gold.fct_sales. The source CSV contained 15 duplicate order records that were not deduplicated before aggregation.
root_cause: Upstream data extract produced duplicate records due to a retry in the source system's export job. No deduplication step existed in the silver layer. The duplicates passed through to fct_sales and inflated daily_revenue by approximately 12%.
evidence_summary: SQL duplicate check found 15 order_ids appearing more than once in bronze.sales. Row count check showed 35 rows instead of expected 20. Metric comparison showed daily_revenue 12% higher than previous day's pattern.
fix_applied: Added DISTINCT ON (order_id) to silver_sales.sql to deduplicate by order_id, keeping the first occurrence. Added unique dbt test on silver.silver_sales.order_id.
prevention_added: Added row count anomaly alert that triggers when bronze row count deviates more than 20% from the 7-day rolling average. Added duplicate check as a dbt test on bronze source.

summary: Source system changed customer_id format from integer to string (e.g., 'CUST-001' instead of '1'). The silver_customers model failed with invalid input syntax for type integer when attempting to cast the new format.
root_cause: Upstream CRM system migrated to a new ID format without notifying the data team. The bronze layer ingested the new format successfully since all columns are TEXT. The silver_customers model has CAST(customer_id AS INTEGER) which fails on non-numeric strings.
evidence_summary: Airflow log showed 'invalid input syntax for type integer: CUST-001'. INFORMATION_SCHEMA confirmed customer_id column exists as TEXT in bronze. Sample rows from bronze.customers showed CUST-001 format. Previous metadata snapshot showed integer values.
fix_applied: Replaced CAST(customer_id AS INTEGER) with a regex-based safe extraction that strips the 'CUST-' prefix and casts the numeric portion. Added schema contract test on bronze source to detect format changes.
prevention_added: Added schema drift detection alert that compares current column value patterns against stored snapshots. Added notification to data team Slack channel on source schema changes.

summary: New column 'discount_pct' added to upstream source but not reflected in bronze table definition or dbt model. Bronze ingestion failed because CSV had 7 columns but table expected 6.
root_cause: Source system added a discount_pct column to the sales export. The bronze.sales table was created with a fixed 6-column schema. The COPY command failed because the CSV header count did not match the table column count.
evidence_summary: Airflow log showed 'extra data after last expected column' error from COPY command. CSV file inspection showed 7 columns with new discount_pct as the last column. INFORMATION_SCHEMA showed bronze.sales has 6 columns.
fix_applied: Added discount_pct TEXT column to bronze.sales table. Updated silver_sales.sql to include CAST(discount_pct AS NUMERIC) with COALESCE default of 0. Updated fct_sales to apply discount in revenue calculation.
prevention_added: Added pre-ingestion CSV header validation that compares incoming column count and names against expected schema. Alert fires on any mismatch before data is loaded.

summary: Missing daily partition for 2026-03-04 in bronze.sales caused zero-row join result for that date in gold.fct_sales. Pipeline completed successfully but produced incomplete output.
root_cause: The source system's daily export for 2026-03-04 failed silently on their end. The CSV file was delivered with only a header row and no data rows. Bronze ingestion succeeded with 0 rows for that date. All downstream models ran without error but produced no data for March 4th.
evidence_summary: SQL partition check showed 0 rows for order_date = '2026-03-04' in bronze.sales. Row count showed 16 total rows instead of expected 20. fct_sales had no rows for that date. Source CSV for that date contained only the header row.
fix_applied: Added completeness check after ingestion that verifies expected date range has non-zero row counts. Investigation with source team identified and fixed their export issue.
prevention_added: Added zero-row partition alert that fires when any expected date has 0 rows after ingestion. Added source system health check as a sensor task before ingestion.

summary: dbt model silver_sales referenced a deleted staging table 'stg_sales' that was removed during a refactor. Model compilation failed with 'relation stg_sales does not exist'.
root_cause: A developer renamed stg_sales to bronze.sales as part of a naming convention refactor but did not update all model references. The silver_sales.sql model still contained a reference to the old table name in a commented-out CTE that was accidentally uncommented during a merge conflict resolution.
evidence_summary: Airflow log showed 'relation public.stg_sales does not exist'. dbt manifest showed silver_sales depends_on includes stg_sales. Code inspection of silver_sales.sql found reference to stg_sales in line 8.
fix_applied: Updated the reference in silver_sales.sql from stg_sales to source('bronze', 'sales'). Removed the orphaned CTE.
prevention_added: Added CI check that runs dbt compile on all models before merge. Added dbt source freshness check to catch broken references early.

summary: LEFT JOIN in fct_sales without null filter produced NULL customer records in the gold layer. Run succeeded but 8 rows had NULL customer_name and NULL region, causing downstream dashboard to show 'Unknown' segments.
root_cause: The fct_sales model uses LEFT JOIN between silver_sales and silver_customers. When customer_id values in silver_sales have no matching record in silver_customers (due to late-arriving dimension data), the join produces NULL values for customer_name and region. No COALESCE or filter was applied.
evidence_summary: SQL null check found 8 rows with NULL customer_name in gold.fct_sales. Code inspection showed LEFT JOIN without COALESCE on customer columns. silver_customers had 5 records but silver_sales referenced 8 distinct customer_ids.
fix_applied: Added COALESCE(sc.customer_name, 'Unknown') and COALESCE(sc.region, 'Unknown') to fct_sales.sql. Added a data quality test that checks for NULL dimension values in the gold layer.
prevention_added: Added referential integrity test between silver_sales.customer_id and silver_customers.customer_id. Added late-arriving dimension handling process.

summary: Database role 'airflow' was missing SELECT grant on the newly created bronze schema after a database migration. All dbt models failed with 'permission denied for schema bronze'.
root_cause: A DBA ran a schema migration that recreated the bronze schema with updated permissions but forgot to re-grant SELECT access to the airflow role. The default schema permissions in PostgreSQL do not automatically carry over when a schema is dropped and recreated.
evidence_summary: Airflow log showed 'permission denied for schema bronze'. INFORMATION_SCHEMA query confirmed bronze schema exists. pg_catalog check showed airflow role missing USAGE and SELECT grants on bronze schema.
fix_applied: Executed GRANT USAGE ON SCHEMA bronze TO airflow and GRANT SELECT ON ALL TABLES IN SCHEMA bronze TO airflow. Updated the migration script to include grant statements.
prevention_added: Added permission validation step in the database migration runbook. Added pre-flight check in the DAG that verifies schema access before running dbt models.

summary: Wrong aggregation grain in fct_sales produced doubled revenue metrics. The model grouped by customer_id and order_date but missed the product dimension, causing multiple orders per customer per day to be collapsed incorrectly.
root_cause: The fct_sales GROUP BY clause included customer_id and order_date but omitted product. When a customer had multiple orders for different products on the same day, the SUM(total_amount) was correct but the COUNT(*) inflated the order_count. The daily_revenue appeared doubled because the join produced a cartesian product with the missing group key.
evidence_summary: Metric comparison showed daily_revenue 2x expected for dates with multi-product orders. Row count showed 15 rows in fct_sales instead of expected 20. SQL check confirmed customers with multiple products per day were collapsed into single rows.
fix_applied: Added product to the GROUP BY clause in fct_sales.sql. Updated the model to aggregate at the correct grain: customer_id, order_date, product.
prevention_added: Added grain validation test that checks row count against expected grain. Added metric anomaly detection comparing current aggregates against 7-day rolling average.

summary: Large unrestricted join between silver_sales (2M rows) and silver_customers (500K rows) without partition pruning caused out-of-memory error on the Airflow worker. Task was killed after exceeding the 2GB memory limit.
root_cause: The fct_sales model performs a full join between silver_sales and silver_customers without any date-based partition filter. As data volume grew from 20 rows in development to 2M rows in production, the join exceeded available memory on the Airflow worker container which has a 2GB memory limit.
evidence_summary: Airflow log showed 'Worker received SIGKILL, likely OOM'. Task instance showed 3 retries, all killed. Docker stats confirmed memory spike to 2GB during join execution. silver_sales row count was 2M, a 100x increase from the previous week due to a historical backfill.
fix_applied: Added date range filter to fct_sales.sql that processes only the last 7 days of data. Increased Airflow worker memory limit to 4GB. Added incremental materialization to process only new data.
prevention_added: Added memory usage monitoring on Airflow workers with alert at 80% threshold. Added row count trend alert to detect unexpected data volume increases. Added incremental processing as the default materialization strategy for large models.
